response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates exceptional software engineering fundamentals with 20 years of experience and proven expertise in distributed systems and high-performance architectures, there is a critical mismatch on the primary technical requirement: C++ experience is minimal (one 6-month role at National Instruments in 2014-15, not in current skills list), and the resume shows no industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or cybersecurity domain knowledge required for this role. Strong general capabilities cannot compensate for the absence of the core language expertise and domain specialization needed.
strengths: Proven mastery of distributed systems, event-driven architectures, and high-performance applications (Dunnhumby: millions of requests/second; GISCollective platform design)
Senior/Staff-level track record with strong software craftsmanship foundations (TDD, CI/CD, pair programming, mentoring, technical communication)
Demonstrated ability to rapidly learn new languages and technologies across 10+ languages and domains (JavaScript, TypeScript, DLang, Java, C#, PHP)
concerns: C++ expertise is critically under-developed: only ~6 months of professional experience (2014-15) and omitted from skills list; no recent projects in the primary language required
Complete absence of industrial automation, embedded systems, or IoT domain experience; all background is web/backend services unrelated to manufacturing or control systems
No evidence of secure development practices, SDL implementation, or industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443); no mention of MQTT, message brokers, or industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850)

key_factors.factor: Industrial domain and embedded systems background
General software engineering excellence and adaptability
